behaviors.description: `=SUM(A1:A10)` evaluates to the correct sum for cells A1..A10.
behaviors.justification: The real failure boundary is the handoff between formula parsing, range resolution (`A1:A10`), and SUM execution. A pure unit test of SUM logic can miss range-binding bugs, off-by-one range expansion, or type coercion issues in wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Create an in-memory workbook, set `A1..A10` to known numeric values, set another cell to `=SUM(A1:A10)`, run evaluation, assert exact numeric result. Include a mixed numeric type case (ints/floats) to catch coercion bugs.

behaviors.description: A formula referencing a cell that itself contains a formula returns the correct cascaded result.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is about cross-cell formula chaining (`A1 -> B1 -> C1`), which depends on evaluator + dependency resolution interaction. Unit tests on a single expression function can miss propagation/order defects.
behaviors.test_description: Set `A1=5`, `B1==A1*2`, `C1==B1+1`; evaluate workbook and assert `B1=10`, `C1=11`. Re-run after changing `A1` and assert updated cascade to verify wiring, not just one-shot evaluation.

behaviors.description: Circular dependency (A1 <-> B1) is detected and reported as circular-reference error.
behaviors.justification: The failure appears when dependency graph construction and evaluator cycle handling interact. Unit tests of only graph cycle detection or only evaluator loop guards can miss integration defects in actual cell reference wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Create `A1==B1+1` and `B1==A1+1`, trigger evaluation, assert a stable circular-reference error object/code (not timeout, hang, or stack overflow). Add assertion that engine remains responsive for subsequent non-circular evaluations.

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP exact match returns correct value; missing key returns error.
behaviors.justification: Real failures often occur at lookup-range parsing, table indexing, and error propagation boundaries, not only inside the VLOOKUP algorithm. Mocking surrounding formula plumbing can hide incorrect range extraction or error mapping.
behaviors.test_description: Populate a lookup table range in cells, evaluate formula using exact-match VLOOKUP for an existing key and a missing key. Assert returned value for hit and specific not-found error for miss.

behaviors.justification: This behavior is primarily expression semantics (nested branching and comparison), and can be caught within a local evaluator boundary without external I/O or cross-module state interactions.
behaviors.test_description: Directly evaluate `=IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")` with parameterized `A1` values (e.g., 5, 15, 25) and assert outputs `low`, `medium`, `high`.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins values with separator and skips empty cells.
behaviors.justification: The core failure boundary is function semantics (argument handling/empty-cell policy), which is local logic. No real infra, subprocess, or multi-module state interaction is required.
behaviors.test_description: Call/evaluate CONCATENATE with representative arguments including empty cells/null-equivalent values and separators; assert output string excludes empty entries rather than inserting extra separators/blank tokens.

behaviors.description: Cross-sheet reference (`Sheet2!A1`) resolves when referenced sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly a module-boundary behavior: sheet namespace resolution + cell evaluator interaction. Unit-testing only arithmetic or only parser logic can miss workbook/sheet lookup wiring bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook with `Sheet1` and `Sheet2`; set `Sheet2!A1`, set `Sheet1!A1==Sheet2!A1+1`, evaluate, assert correct result. Include a control where sheet name differs only by case if case-sensitivity rules matter.

behaviors.description: Dependent formulas recalculate in correct topological order after referenced value change.
behaviors.justification: The failure depends on stateful interaction among dependency graph update, invalidation, and scheduler/evaluator ordering. A unit test of one formula function cannot expose stale-value or wrong-order recomputation bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Build a dependency chain/diamond graph (e.g., `A1` feeds `B1` and `C1`, both feed `D1`), evaluate once, mutate `A1`, trigger recalc, assert all downstream cells recompute to expected values and no stale intermediate remains.

behaviors.description: Undo after formula edit restores formula text and prior dependent computed values.
behaviors.justification: Undo correctness here spans undo/redo state stack plus recomputation engine. Real failures emerge from module interaction and state restoration, not isolated function logic.
behaviors.test_description: Start with baseline formulas and computed dependents; edit one upstream formula; verify changed outputs; perform undo; assert restored formula text and all dependent cell values exactly match pre-edit snapshot.

behaviors.description: Save/load round-trip preserves formulas, values, and floating-point precision.
behaviors.justification: The behavior requires real serializer/deserializer + local filesystem round-trip. Mocked persistence can hide binary format and precision defects. It does not require external network/subprocess, so System is higher than necessary.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook with representative formulas and challenging float values, save to a temp file in the real custom binary format, reload, and assert formula text equality plus numeric equality under strict precision policy (bitwise or defined epsilon).
